title: The Cold Hearth
subtitle: Chapter 02 · The Wider Fractures
introduction: A fireplace in an abandoned cottage is covered in winter ash. Focus on the pine log.
description: A fireplace in an abandoned cottage is covered in winter ash. Focus on the pine log.
observation: A dry pine log is placed on the cold ashes, immediately radiating warm heat waves before the match is struck.
reconstruction: Carry the unlit pine log, the unstruck match, and the cold stone hearth floor.
attunements: The pine bark is dry, yet radiating 120 degrees of dry heat.
The phosphorus head of the unstruck match is pristine and cold.
The stone hearth floor is warm to the touch, carrying no ash soot.
revelation:
What was wrong: the room gathered the warmth of a fire that had not yet been lit.

Why it mattered: the hearth was keeping the memory of winter comfort alive, projecting its warmth backward into the cold cottage.

anomaly_definition.misstep_text: Not there. Focus on the dry pine log, not the cold stones.
anomaly_definition.success_text: Thermal blueshift located. Heat waves rise from unlit wood.

capture_window.guidance_text: HOLD WHEN THE THERMAL VIBRATIONS EMBARK.
capture_window.success_text: Timeline isolated: thermal waves precede match combustion.

evidence_nodes.relevance: THE PINE BARK IS DRY AND RADIATING HEAT. Waves of dry warmth rise from the cold logs in the grate.
THE MATCH HEAD IS PRISTINE AND COLD. The match remains unburned, holding the potential flame in suspension.
THE STONE FLOOR IS WARM TO THE TOUCH. The ash hearth is fully heated before receiving any combustion flame.
